title: pulicaria vulgaris subsp. pomeliana
intro: Pulicaria vulgaris subsp. pomeliana is a subspecies of the bitter lettuce plant, a perennial herb native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, as well as its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The health benefits of Pulicaria vulgaris subsp. pomeliana are varied, including its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
